title: Cracking the code: social data access and the DSA's impact on disinformation research
snippet: This article, co-authored by Fabio Giglietto, discusses the impact of the Digital Services Act (DSA) on disinformation research. It examines the challenges and opportunities presented by the DSA in terms of accessing social media data for studying disinformation, algorithmic bias, and online manipulation. It is part of the PROMPT series.
description: This article discusses the impact of the Digital Services Act (DSA) on disinformation research and is co-authored by Fabio Giglietto, focusing on challenges and opportunities for accessing social media data for studying disinformation and online manipulation.

title: Health Discourse Research Initiative
snippet: Fabio Giglietto, University of Urbino Carlo Bo, is mentioned as part of a team working to identify coordinated link-sharing networks on Facebook, focusing on health information in Nigeria and India. The research used the CooRnet package on CrowdTangle data.
description: The Health Discourse Research Initiative mentions Fabio Giglietto, a professor at the University of Urbino Carlo Bo, as part of a team studying coordinated link-sharing networks on Facebook related to health information using the CooRnet package on CrowdTangle data.

title: Who is behind vera.ai? Meet the people and teams that make up the vera.ai project. #8 Team of University of Urbino Carlo Bo
snippet: An interview with Fabio Giglietto, an Associate Professor at the Department of Communication Sciences, Humanities, and International Studies, and coordinator of the Mapping Italian News Research Program at the University of Urbino Carlo Bo, along with Roberto Mincigrucci and Giada Marino, about their work on Work Package 4 (WP4) within the vera.ai project, which focuses on discovering potentially problematic content in near-real-time using a content-agnostic approach, specifically addressing coordinated sharing behavior.
description: This web mention features an interview with Fabio Giglietto, an Associate Professor at the University of Urbino Carlo Bo, discussing his work on Work Package 4 (WP4) within the vera.ai project, which focuses on discovering problematic content using a content-agnostic approach.
